RepresentativeInfo.CapitolAddress: MO House of Representatives, 201 West Capitol Avenue, Room 302-A, Jefferson City MO 65101
RepresentativeInfo.Biography:
Representative Alex Riley, a Republican, represents Greene County (District 134) in the Missouri House of Representatives. He was elected to his first two-year term in November 2020.
In addition to his legislative duties, Riley is an attorney with the Healy Law Offices, LLC. He is a member of the Missouri Bar Association, the Missouri Organization of Defense Lawyers, the Springfield Metropolitan Bar Association, and the Network – The Springfield Area Chamber of Commerce’s Young Professionals Organization.
Riley holds a B.A. in political science from Thomas Edison State University, and a Juris Doctorate from Southern Illinois University.
Born and raised in Springfield, Riley resides there with his wife, Ellen, and their three young children.

SponsoredBill.LongTitle: Convenes a joint session for the State of the State Address
Convenes a joint session to receive a message from the Chief Justice of the Supreme Court of Missouri
Temporary Rules of the House of Representatives for the 103rd General Assembly
Informs the Governor and the Senate that the House is convened for the 2025 veto session
Establishes the elected permanent officers of the 103rd General Assembly
Informs the Senate that the House adopted no motions to override the veto of certain bills
Informs the Senate that the House is duly convened, has elected its officers, and is in session ready for consideration of business
Informs the Governor that the 103rd General Assembly, First Regular Session, is organized and ready for business
Expresses appreciation for the Secretary of State
Expresses appreciation for Mary Rhodes Russell of the Missouri Supreme Court for administering the oath
Rules of the House of Representatives for the 103rd General Assembly
Grants the use of the House Chamber to the Jefferson City Chapter of the Federalist Society on February 21, 2025, from 7:30 am to 4:30 pm

CoSponsoredBill.LongTitle: Establishes the "Missouri Religious Freedom Protection Act"
Requires the general assembly to approve proposed administrative rules
Proposes a constitutional amendment establishing provisions relating to reproductive health care
Proposes a constitutional amendment that establishes provisions relating to reproductive health care
